reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: Several pages from the source PDF are entirely missing from the conversion result. Specifically, Pages 6, 7, 8, and 9 (Intelligent Collaboration details), Pages 11, 12, and 13 (Organization benefits and Rich Engagements header), and the majority of the 'Places' features and meeting protections on Pages 15 through 26 are absent. The reading order indices in the JSON also show significant gaps (e.g., from index 56 to 108, and 195 to 321).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains 30 pages of content, including section headers and detailed feature slides.
concerns.output_quote: The output JSON contains an '_omitted' block and gaps in reading_order, leaving out content from approximately 20 pages (6-9, 11-13, 15-26).

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: Within the sections that were successfully converted, there are significant omissions. The email address 'annadockum@microsoft.com' is missing from Page 1. Additionally, one of the three references at the bottom of Page 4 ('Microsoft Work Trend Index: Annual Report, 2023') is missing. Several explanatory footnotes from the bottom of slides (e.g., Page 17, 21, 25) are also absent.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: annadockum@microsoft.com; Microsoft Work Trend Index: Annual Report, 2023
concerns.output_quote: The email address is missing from the paragraph elements of the first section. The 2023 reference is missing from the paragraph elements at index 38-39.

concerns.analysis: The table structure on Page 28 ('AI capabilities across licenses') is corrupted. In the source document, the table uses spanning rows to define sections ('AI capabilities deeply embedded in Teams', 'Meeting Experience', 'Other capabilities') that should encompass all columns. The output JSON fails to identify these as spanning rows (colspan is 1) and incorrectly merges data from the next row into the remaining columns of the header row. concerns.source_pdf_quote: Slide 28 table 'AI capabilities across licenses' contains section header rows spanning 4 columns (e.g., 'AI capabilities deeply embedded in Teams').
concerns.output_quote: The JSON table (reading_order 332) misidentifies these headers as single-cell data entries and merges them with data from subsequent rows (e.g., row index 1, cell index 1: 'AI-based noise suppression').

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: The table 'AI capabilities across licenses' from Page 28 has a structural defect. The rows contain 5 cells while the header only defines 4. This results in a misalignment where the checkmark symbols (indicating availability) are shifted one column to the right. For example, in the first data row ('AI-based noise suppression'), the checkmark intended for the 'Microsoft Teams' column is placed in the third cell, aligning it with 'Microsoft Teams Premium'. concerns.output_quote: Row 1: ["AI capabilities...", "AI-based noise suppression", "\u2713", "", ""]. Index 2 (3rd cell) aligns with 'Microsoft Teams Premium'.
